channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
item.description: About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life." Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was […]

item.title: On the stand, Elon Musk can’t escape his own tweets
item.description: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day for his attempt to legally dismantle OpenAI.

item.title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR
item.description: Meta is losing billions on Reality Labs each quarter, and its AI expenditures are only going to increase its spending.

item.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
item.description: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

item.title: How Elon Musk Squeezed OpenAI: They 'Are Gonna Want to Kill Me’
item.description: Tensions flared on the third day of trial in Musk v. Altman as OpenAI’s lawyers cross-examined Musk.

item.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
item.description: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta is working on AI agents for personal and business use
item.description: The CEO wants to make an agent so easy even his mom can use it.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Thursday, April 30 (game #1557)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Thursday, April 30 (game #1054)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Thursday, April 30 (game #788)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Vous avez connu cet écran noir dans les années 90 ? Lenovo vient de le racheter
item.description: Lenovo vient de boucler le rachat de Phoenix Technologies, l'un des trois grands noms du BIOS depuis 1979. Une acquisition discrète, mais qui en dit long sur la bataille que se livrent les fabricants de PC à l'heure de l'intelligence artificielle embarquée.

item.title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
item.description: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

item.title: 'The era of the pilot is over, the era of the agent is here': Google Cloud wants you to unlock the power of your data
item.description: Google Cloud knows most of your data is unstructured and lives in other environments – but it still wants to help you tap into the huge potential.

item.title: Prompts now, pollutants later: Report claims data centers are harming the environment to the tune of $25 billion and inducing a debt on the health of current and future generations
item.description: A new paper theorizes that the cost of gross external damages generated by data centers could exceed $25 billion.
